title: Nursery Rhyme. CLXXII. Songs.
content:
[The original of the following is to be found in 'Deuteromelia, or the second part of Musicks Melodie,' 4to, Lond. 1609, where the music is also given.]

        Three blind mice, see how they run!
        They all ran after the farmer's wife,
        Who cut off their tails with the carving-knife,
        Did you ever see such fools in your life?
        Three blind mice.
meaning:
Unknown's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Nursery Rhyme. CLXXII. Songs."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
